name: Mercer
description_short: Mercer Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative serving Mercer County since 1969, offering savings accounts, CDs, and loans to eligible employees and their families.
description_long:
Mercer Credit Union has operated as a community-focused credit union in Mercer County, Illinois since 1969. The institution serves a defined membership base primarily composed of employees from Rockridge, Sherrard, and Mercer County school districts, along with Mercer County and City of Aledo employees, healthcare workers in Mercer County facilities, and immediate family members of current members. This membership restriction keeps the credit union closely tied to its local community.

The credit union offers interest-bearing share savings accounts and certificates of deposit (CDs) as their primary deposit products. Share savings accounts earn dividends computed and deposited quarterly, with rates determined by the Board of Directors each dividend period. Their 12-month CD requires a $1,000 minimum deposit and carries an early withdrawal penalty. As of February 20, 2026, they offer a Share Savings rate of 0.75% and a 12-month CD rate of 3.5%. They also maintain a Christmas Club account earning 1.50% with checks mailed on November 1st annually. Beyond savings products, they provide personal loans to members, though specific terms are not detailed on their website.

Mercer Credit Union distinguishes itself through its long community history, membership-based cooperative structure, and local presence in Mercer County. Operating from the Mercer County Courthouse in Aledo, they maintain limited office hours (Monday, Wednesday, Friday 9am-4pm) reflecting their small-scale operation. The credit union offers a scholarship application program for members' families, demonstrating community investment beyond basic financial services.

The primary limitation is that Mercer Credit Union serves a restricted membership—you must be employed by specific organizations or be related to an existing member. Their limited office hours and single physical location make accessibility challenging for non-local members. The website provides minimal detail about loan products, terms, or rates, and rates on savings products are relatively modest compared to some online alternatives. This institution is best suited for eligible employees within their service area seeking straightforward, community-based banking. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Share savings accounts with quarterly dividend payments
Local in-person banking at Mercer County Courthouse
pros: Long-established credit union with 50+ years of community service since 1969
Member-owned cooperative structure aligns institution incentives with member interests
Quarterly dividend payments on share savings accounts
CD rates competitive at 3.5% for 12-month terms (as of Feb 2026)
Offers member scholarship programs for community investment
No membership fees mentioned; basic banking without profit-driven fees
Direct relationship with local community through courthouse location
cons: Restricted membership—only eligible if employed by specific organizations or related to current members
Very limited office hours (Monday, Wednesday, Friday 9am-4pm only, closed 12-1pm lunch)
Single physical location in Aledo, Illinois limits accessibility
Share savings rate of 0.75% is below many online banking alternatives
Website lacks detailed information on loan products, rates, terms, and application process
Dividends not guaranteed and depend on earnings; members forfeit dividends if account closed before credit
best_for: Employees of Rockridge, Sherrard, or Mercer County school districts seeking local banking
Mercer County and City of Aledo government employees and their families
Healthcare workers employed at Mercer County health facilities
Immediate family members of existing Mercer Credit Union members
diagnosis: Mercer Credit Union is best for employees within its defined membership groups who value community-based banking and member ownership over rates and convenience. The critical caveat is membership eligibility—if you don't work for one of their specified employers or know a member, you cannot join, making this institution inaccessible to most consumers.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
